channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: Peppa Pig and Transformers owner Hasbro hit by cyber-attack
item.description: [BBC Technology] The firm says its operations remain open but says the hack "may result in some delays".

item.title: The real impact of AI on budgets, stacks and teams
item.description: [MarTech] AI is replacing tools and reshaping budgets, but it’s also expanding stacks and increasing headcount. The post The real impact of AI on budgets, stacks and teams appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Agentic AI discovery requires machine-readable brands
item.description: [MarTech] Drive better visibility and outcomes in AI search by making your brand easier for machines to interpret, connect and act on. The post Agentic AI discovery requires machine-readable brands appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Claude Code users hitting usage limits 'way faster than expected'
item.description: [BBC Technology] Anthropic, the company behind the AI coding assistant, said it was fixing a problem blocking users.

item.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
item.description: [BBC Technology] Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

item.title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
item.description: [BBC Technology] It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

item.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
item.description: [The Verge AI] If you use the AI-powered note-taking app Granola, you might want to double-check your privacy settings. Though Granola says your notes are "private by default," it makes them viewable to anyone with a link, and also uses them for internal AI training unless you opt out. Granola describes itself as

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran’s Threats on US Tech, Trump’s Plans for Midterms, and Polymarket’s Pop-up Flop
item.description: [WIRED AI] In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.

item.title: OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
item.description: [WIRED AI] OpenAI is acquiring TBPN, a business talk show that’s popular among Silicon Valley elites, as it continues to battle its negative public image.

item.title: OpenAI acquires technology talk show TBPN in surprise move
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant
item.description: [WIRED AI] Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.

item.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
item.description: [The Verge AI] To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should all mark our calendars for June 7th as an IPO date just in case. Based on the April […]

item.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
item.description: [The Verge AI] OpenAI has purchased TBPN, an online talk show that often interviews AI executives and other tech leaders. The show goes live every weekday at 2PM PT, often for a three-hour duration, counting OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, as well as executives from Meta, Microsoft, Palantir, and Andreessen Horowitz, among

item.title: The latest AI-powered martech news and releases
item.description: [MarTech] AI chatbots are now ignoring instructions and going off-script — because apparently even automation tools can get bored with their jobs. The post The latest AI-powered martech news and releases appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Fewer UK adults posting on social media, Ofcom finds
item.description: [BBC Technology] Some experts believe it highlights a social media shift as platforms boost short video.

item.title: HubSpot moves to outcome-based pricing for some Breeze AI agents
item.description: [MarTech] HubSpot bets customers will be more likely to deploy its AI agents if they only pay when the agent does its job. The post HubSpot moves to outcome-based pricing for some Breeze AI agents appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: As AI does more of the work, are we building the right leaders?
item.description: [MarTech] AI is accelerating analysis and reporting, but fewer marketers understand the data underneath. That gap is where leadership risk begins. The post As AI does more of the work, are we building the right leaders? appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to be worth $1 trillion with planned public listing
item.description: [BBC Technology] The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

item.title: Singapore’s deep-tech push requires ‘calculated bets’, stronger ecosystem: NUS Enterprise’s Benjamin Tee
item.description: [Business Times] Such ventures are not about moving from ‘zero to one’, but about scaling breakthroughs to impact millions, he says

item.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: [WIRED AI] Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

item.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
item.description: [The Verge AI] OpenAI is undergoing another round of C-suite changes, according to an internal memo viewed by The Verge. Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment - who was until recently the company's CEO of applications - says in the memo that she will be stepping away on medical leave "for the next several wee

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

item.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

item.title: OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
item.description: [WIRED AI] The company is undergoing major leadership restructuring as its CEO of AGI deployment goes on leave for “several weeks.”

item.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

item.title: Musk asks SpaceX IPO banks to buy Grok AI subscriptions, NYT reports
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: US judge upholds block on subpoenas to Fed’s Jerome Powell, teeing up likely appeal
item.description: [Straits Times] An appeal could delay confirmation of Mr Kevin Warsh, Mr Trump’s pick to succeed Mr Powell as Fed chair.

item.title: US registers strong job growth in March; unemployment rate at 4.3%
item.description: [Straits Times] The world’s largest economy gained 178,000 jobs in March, after losing 133,000 in February.

item.title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Moonbounce has raised $12 million to grow its AI control engine that converts content moderation policies into consistent, predictable AI behavior.

item.title: Trump administration proposes expanding Chinese tech gear crackdown
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Apple’s best product ever
item.description: [The Verge AI] All week, we've been asking you to help us rank the 50 best products Apple ever made, as we mark the company's 50th anniversary. Thanks to everyone who pitched in - we ended up with more than 1.6 million votes! We also have lots of other coverage of Apple's first half century, and you should [&#8230

item.title: Your AI governance gap is bigger than you think
item.description: [MarTech] AI is already being used across your organization. Audit tools, set guardrails and reduce risk with a practical governance framework. The post Your AI governance gap is bigger than you think appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: 神州数码2025年营收超1400亿，AI相关业务增长近五成｜最前线
item.description: [36氪] 作者｜黄楠 编辑｜袁斯来 神州数码（000034.SZ）2025年全年业绩报告近日发布。 财报显示，公司全年实现营业收入1438亿元，同比增长12%，营收规模连续三年稳步攀升。其中，AI相关业务成为核心增长引擎，全年收入达330亿元，同比增长了48%，正式迈入规模化落地期，成为与传统分销业务并重的当期增长极。 具体到业务板块，高价值战略业务呈现爆发式增长。以神州问学为核心的AI软件及服务业务营收1.1亿元，同比增长了165.4%，企业级Agent中台进入商业化变现阶段；数云服务及软件业务营收35.6亿元，同比增长22%；自有品牌神州鲲泰算力产品营收同比增长62.4%达到74.4

item.title: 微医智能体为医保基金装上“数字天眼”，AI守护百姓“救命钱、保命钱”
item.description: [钛媒体] 以AI为“盾”守护民生底线，以“锚”的定力锚定行业方向，微医用技术筑牢医保基金安全防线，让医保监管更精准、医疗服务更高效、群众就医更安心。

item.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
item.description: [The Verge AI] Utah is allowing an AI system to prescribe psychiatric drugs without a doctor. It's only the second time the state - and the country - has delegated this kind of clinical authority to AI. State officials say it could bring costs down and ease care shortages, but physicians warn the system is opaque,

item.title: The 3 Best Portable Jump Starters in 2026: Get Charged Up
item.description: [WIRED AI] The new crop of portable jump starters means you'll never be stuck waiting on a tow. Here are the devices we'd trust.

item.title: AI的「第一杯咖啡」：当具身智能走进工厂，人机协作的边界在哪里？
item.description: [钛媒体] 让AI写一首诗很容易，但让它去车间稳稳当当地拧好一颗螺丝，至今还是个难题。当大模型走出屏幕、触摸现实，具身智能的"第一杯咖啡"究竟是什么味道？

item.title: 当Token成为产业共识，火山引擎的好消息和坏消息
item.description: [钛媒体] 一年前，谭待曾用马拉松作比喻，AI行业大概跑了500米，现在，他判断这个距离变成了一公里。

item.title: 三万亿美元IPO即将上演，市场在激动同时也在颤栗
item.description: [钛媒体] OpenAI、SpaceX、Anthropic箭在弦上

item.title: 阿里AI的势能，又起来了
item.description: [钛媒体] 千问4天模型三连发，信号更在“模型”外。

item.title: 奥特曼预言与现实相差几何？12个顶级模型“创业”一年，仅3个存活
item.description: [钛媒体] AI能帮你经营一家创业公司吗？

item.title: Impact of Iran war contained, but S’poreans starting to feel pinch
item.description: [Straits Times] PM Lawrence Wong said some support measures will be brought forward, to provide earlier relief.

item.title: Hyundai Motor flags export disruptions as Middle East conflict hits shipping
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Airbus bets on helicopter capability for tomorrow's war drones
item.description: [Straits Times] Airbus’ strategy essentially involves fusing cooperation on drones and helicopters.

item.title: Microsoft readies US$10 billion AI investment plan in Japan
item.description: [Straits Times] It will develop cloud and AI infrastructure alongside Sakura Internet and telecom operator SoftBank

item.title: 阅文“AI拟真人大讲堂”：好故事才是AI时代的未来
item.description: [36氪] 如今内容行业风头正盛的莫过于AI拟真人剧。 它是从AI漫剧这一赛道中生长出来的内容形态，也是视频生成大模型飞跃式进步下的产物。 早在2025年下半年的时候，这一内容形态就已经崭露头角。彼时有一批从业者认为，AI拟真人剧的天花板相当于整个传统影视行业，要高于AI漫剧，于是在诸多技术问题尚未被解决的时候就决定入局。 今年1月以来，字节旗下Seedance2.0、生数科技Vidu Q3的发布则彻底引爆了这一赛道。人物表情的恐怖谷效应、角色难以保持的一致性，以及打斗场面不连贯等问题，都在底层大模型技术的进步中消融。 Vidu解决方案负责人郑重如此表示，视频生成大模型在过去一年中，从“老

item.title: Secret codes and renminbi fees get ships through Iran’s Hormuz tollbooth
item.description: [Straits Times] Iran’s National Security Committee has approved a Bill that would impose fees on Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 36氪首发丨前赛默飞员工创业黑灯实验室，实现国内100%交付，获近亿融资
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨欧雪 编辑丨袁斯来 硬氪获悉，北京戴纳实验科技股份有限公司（下称“戴纳科技”）已于近日完成近亿元B+轮融资。本轮由顺禧基金管理的北京市新材料产业投资基金（以下简称“北京新材料基金”）和北京市丰台区丰泽志远产业培育基金（以下简称“丰泽基金”）共同投资。资金将主要用于核心技术迭代、产能扩张，以及与北京化工大学联合搭建全球首个新材料CRO平台，加速AI+新材料研发服务的产业化落地。 戴纳科技成立于2011年，是国内首家提出并100%成功交付黑灯实验室的企业。公司专注于全球AI4S（AI for Science）基础设施的建设，通过构建以“AI+黑灯实验室”为基础的通用研发平台实现“

item.title: Microsoft to invest $10 billion in Japan for AI and cyber defence expansion
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: 36氪首发 | 00后上交医学博士做了款可穿戴止鼾设备，获得顺为独家投资
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 硬氪获悉，深圳夜合科技有限公司（下称“夜合科技”）近日宣布完成千万元天使轮融资，由顺为资本独家投资，深渡资本担任本轮融资的财务顾问。资金主要用于核心产品研发，加速“医疗技术向消费场景延伸”。 夜合科技成立于2025年，总部位于深圳。首款产品聚焦睡眠呼吸赛道，为鼾声人群和阻塞性睡眠呼吸暂停（OSA）患者开发轻量化、舒适、无体位限制的可穿戴颈部设备。 公司创始人冉昕昕拥有华西、瑞金两大顶级医院的医学背景，于上海交通大学攻读医学博士学位，长期专注于医工交叉领域的研究。在临床实践中，冉昕昕发现患者缺乏消费级止鼾

item.title: 叽伴：用「共同经历」重新定义AI社交
item.description: [36氪] 随着大语言模型和各类Agent的崛起，国内外涌现出一批以AI为核心的社交产品。然而在行业热闹背后，一个尴尬的现实是：用户尝鲜热情高，留存率却惨淡。 聊得越久，越发现角色“失忆”——上周提过的重要事情这周就忘；OOC（性格漂移）——原本高冷的角色突然变得谄媚讨好；最终演变成编剧疲劳的独角戏，用户既要当体验者又要当编剧，负担过重，自然流失。 技术越进步，用户对虚拟陪伴的期待反而越现实：要的不是更会说话的AI，而是一段经得起时间、有厚度、有记忆的关系。换句话说，AI社交的本质，不是更智能的对话，而是可沉淀的关系。 前不久开启公测的AI社交产品「叽伴」，便是为了解决这些痛点而来。

item.title: 36氪首发 | 3个月融资近10亿，深圳量子计算公司完成C+轮融资，并已规模化盈利
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨欧雪 编辑丨袁斯来 硬氪获悉，国内量子计算企业——量旋科技（SpinQ）已于近日完成C+轮融资，金额达6亿元。若将三个月前完成的C轮融资合并计算，公司在短短一个季度内累计融资近10亿元。 本轮融资汇集了多元化的投资阵容，既包括基石资本等知名投资机构，也有国泰君安创新投等国央企背景的机构，以及浙江财通资本、四川振兴集团、成都天创投等地方投资平台，毅达资本、华强资本持续跟投。 量旋科技成立于2018年，是一家专注于量子计算产业化的高科技企业，总部位于深圳。公司创始人兼CEO项金根毕业于清华大学物理系，曾任哈佛大学博士后研究员，在量子计算领域拥有超过15年的研发经验。核心团队来
